    • Version 2.2 Last Modified: November 3, 2022

Security Assessment at First Entry

SAFE is designed to assess the current security posture and identify options for facility owners and operators to mitigate against relevant threats. It is not intended to be an in-depth security assessment.

SAFE may be the first step toward an effective security program. It is generally intended for facilities that have limited security measures or planning in place at the time of the assessment. In the future, it may be appropriate to conduct a more detailed assessment, particularly after additional security measures have been implemented onsite.

Instructions:

SAFE performs best in Microsoft Edge on a computer running Windows 7 (or later) and with Excel 97 (or later).

Click here to open the Safe User Manual (.pdf)

1. If an alert displays stating that your browser has “restricted the webpage from running scripts or ActiveX controls,” click the “Allow blocked content” button. If you miss this step, refresh the browser page to display the alert again.
 
2. Fill out the “SAFE Visit Information” tab to create a new SAFE, or load a previously saved SAFE to continue working on it. Then click the “Continue with this SAFE” button.

- To continue working on an existing SAFE, click the “Browse” button and locate the appropriate .txt file. Then click the “Load saved data” button.
3. Click the tabs on the left to view and select standard vulnerabilities and options for consideration (VOFCs) for each category.

- Click the plus sign (+) to expand categories and VOFCs.

- Check the box(es) to select any desired OFCs.

- Click the minus sign (-) to collapse categories and VOFCs.

- Click the “Clear VOFCs” at the top of each section to clear all of the selected VOFCs in that category.

- Click the “Clear All Data” button in the top left corner of the browser to delete all of the information entered for this SAFE.
4. Click the “Custom VOFCs” tab to add up to 15 customized VOFCs. (*optional*)

- NOTE: You must select a category for each custom VOFC by using the “Category” drop-down menu.

- To copy a standard vulnerability and add a custom OFC for it, click the “Copy Vulnerability Text” button to the right of that vulnerability. Then paste the text into the “Vulnerability” field on the “Custom VOFCs” tab.
5. To add Commendables, click the “Commendable Action and Practices” tab and click the plus sign (+) to expand each category. (*optional*)
 
6. Click the “Additional Points of Contact” tab to add contact information for up to 10 individuals, agencies, or organizations. (*optional*)
 
7. Click the “Selected VOFCs” tab at any time to view the selected VOFCs.

- Custom VOFCs, if any, are highlighted yellow.

- Click the “Print/Create PDF” button to download the VOFCs into a printable PDF file. NOTE: Doing so will not generate the entire report.

- Click the “Export to Excel” button to create an editable spreadsheet of the VOFCs. Instructions on formatting this file are available at the bottom of this page.
8. Click the “Create Report” tab to view and export the report.

- To print the report, right-click anywhere on this page, or in the upper right corner, click the icon that allows printing.

- From the menu that appears, select “Print.”

- We recommend choosing the "Save As PDF" printer to convert the file to PDF as this provides the best layout and keeps text as text vs converting everything to a graphic.

- Suggested Naming Convention: “Facility Name_SAFE Report_MMDDYYYY” Example: XYZChurch_SAFE Report_03192020

- WARNING: Edits to the content cannot be made within the PDF if you use Adobe PDF printer from the browser print dialog. If you prefer to use Adobe, please either use the Adobe browser plug-in, or make any changes via the SAFE tool, and recreate the report.
9. Save data for recordkeeping or future use.

- Click the “Save Data” button in the top left corner of the browser. NOTE: Click this button only when ready to exit the tool—it is not necessary to periodically save data while using the application.

- Click the “Save” button; the data will be saved in a .txt format. This file will not display relevant information when opened with an application like Notepad; however, the SAFE tool will use this file should you choose to load the data and continue working on it.
10. PCII submission

- For information about submitting a SAFE report for PCII protection, visit the CISA website at https://www.cisa.gov/electronic-submit-cii-pcii-protection.

WARNING

This information is FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY (FOUO). It contains information that may be exempt from public release under the Freedom of Information act (5 U.S.C. 552). It is to be controlled, stored, handled, transmitted, distributed, and disposed of in accordance with DHS Management Directive 11042.1 relating to FOUO information and is not to be released to the public or other personnel who do not have a valid "Need-to-know" without prior approval of an authorized DHS official;
For more information about this tool, contact the DHS CISA Field Operations Staff Duty Desk at ISDAssessments@cisa.dhs.gov.

SAFE Information

To begin this SAFE, either browse to a saved data file and click the “Load saved data” button or begin a new SAFE by entering the PSA, Owner/Operator, and Facility information. Once either task is completed, click the “Continue with this SAFE” button.

Selected VOFCs

   


Category Vulnerability Options for Consideration
Blank Blank Blank

   

  • 1. Open the .csv file in Excel.
  • 2. Resize the columns.
  • - Click the triangle in the corner between row 1 and column A.
  • - On the “Home” tab in the “Cells” group, click “Format” and select “AutoFit Column Width.”
  • - Click on Column A to highlight it. On the “Home” tab in the “Cells” group, click “Format” and select “Column Width” and enter 20.
  • - Repeat this process with Column B; set the “Column Width” to 50.
  • - Repeat this process with Column C; set the “Column Width” to 50.
  • 3. Format the columns so text appears on multiple lines in a cell.
  • - Click the triangle in the corner between row 1 and column A.
  • - On the “Home” tab in the “Alignment” group, click “Wrap Text.”
  • 4. Prepare to print.
  • - On the “Page Layout” tab in the “Page Setup” group, click “Orientation” and select “Landscape.”
  • - On the “Page Layout” tab in the “Sheet Options” group, click the expand arrow in the bottom right corner; a “Page Setup” window will display.
  • - On the “Sheet” tab, check the “Gridlines” box.
  • - On the “Page” tab, check the “Fit to” radio button and make sure a 1 displays in the first text field. The second text field should automatically display the number of pages needed for the number of records.
  • - Click the “OK” button.
  • 5. Save the file.
  • - Go to “File,” “Save As,” and choose a location to save the file.
  • - Name the file. Suggested naming convention: “Facility Name_ActionItems_MMDDYYYY”
        Example: XYZ_ActionItems_08012020
  • - Change the “Save as type” drop-down to “Excel Workbook.”
  • - Click the “Save” button.
    •

    SAFE Report


    Warning: Output results are best when using the Edge or Chrome browser.

    Multiple printer options in the printer drop-down menu will create a PDF document from the SAFE interface. However, the “Save as PDF” option produces the best results for the final product.

    Instructions

    • 1. Right-click anywhere on this page, or click the Settings icon in the upper right corner.

    • 2. From the menu that appears, select “Print.”

    • 3. Select “Save as PDF” and click “Save.”

    • 4. Adjust the name if desired, choose a file location, and click "Save.”

    FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY






    SECURITY ASSESSMENT ATFIRST ENTRY








    WARNING
    This information is FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY (FOUO). It contains information that may be exempt from public release under the Freedom of Information act (5 U.S.C. § 552). It is to be controlled, stored, handled, transmitted, distributed, and disposed of in accordance with DHS Management Directive 11042.1 relating to FOUO information and is not to be released to the public or other personnel who do not have a valid “Need-to-know” without prior approval of an authorized DHS official.

    For more information about this document, contact the DHS CISA Field Operations Staff Duty Desk at ISDAssessments@cisa.dhs.gov.



    FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY
     
    FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY
     

    On behalf of the U.S. Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), thank you for your participation in the Security Assessment At First Entry (SAFE). We appreciate the opportunity to work with you through this process to assist your organization in improving its physical and operational security.

    SAFE is designed to assess the current security posture and identify options for facility owners and operators to mitigate against relevant threats. It is not intended to be an in-depth security assessment.

    SAFE may be the first step toward an effective security program. In the future, it may be appropriate to conduct a more detailed assessment, particularly after additional security measures have been implemented onsite.

    CISA conducted a visit on , at . This report lists commendable actions (what the facility is doing well), vulnerabilities (what the facility could improve), and options for consideration (potential security enhancements) based on the Protective Security Advisor's (PSA's) observations and discussions with key site personnel during that visit.

    Findings

    Commendable Actions and Practices

    As part of the onsite visit, the PSA noted several positive and commendable operations-related actions and security practices. The table below lists these observations.


    Commendable Actions and Practices

    Category Commendable Action or Practice
    Blank Blank

    Vulnerabilities and Options for Consideration

    The PSA identified potential vulnerabilities and suggested options the facility may consider to reduce them. The table below lists these vulnerabilities and options for consideration.

    The SAFE process and options for consideration provide an opportunity for the facility to mitigate vulnerabilities and implement protective measures. The options for consideration are not prescriptive endorsements of specific protective measures to be installed and/or used at the facility. The owner or operator determines for the facility whether the options for consideration provide the desired enhancements in light of the facility's current security posture, anticipated growth or organizational changes, budgetary outlook, etc.

    The options for consideration provide actions that may help improve physical and operational security. References for the options for consideration provided below are listed at the end of the report.


    Vulnerabilities and Options for Consideration

    Category Vulnerability Option for Consideration
    Blank Blank Blank


    Conclusion

    This report provides a summary of key findings of the SAFE at your facility and may be used as a guide for the consideration and implementation of future security measures. Please contact your PSA, , or DHS with any inquiries about the information contained in this report:

    • DHS CISA
      Email: ISDAssessments@cisa.dhs.gov
